  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

IPsec API (gxcr/ipsec.h)
[gxcr User-Level Crypto Acceleration API]

Detailed Description

The gxcr_ipsec_ API, declared in gxcr/ipsec.h, provides a basic framework for IPsec processing with the MiCA Packet Processing engine.

It allows the user to set up a Security Association data structure based on the parameters in gxcr_ipsec_params_t. However, the actual packet processing requires the programming of a custom "token" for the engine. The token is a series of instructions, some of which have fields that vary on a per-SA basis, some of which vary on a per-packet basis. Specific examples of this custom layer are in tokens/esp_ipv4_tunnel_ibound.h and tokens/esp_ipv4_tunnel_obound.h. See this example program for more information about how to use the gxcr_ipsec_ API:

  • mica/ipsec/appc : IPsec ESP/IPv4/Tunneling Outbound and Inbound packet processing.

The Packet Processing engine is a very flexible, programmable engine which is capable of many types of IPsec, SSL, and other packet processing. Please read the IO Device Guide (UG404) Appendix D for more information on the programming of the Packet Processing engine.


Data Structures

struct  gxcr_ipsec_params_t
 Parameters used to set up an IPsec SA. More...
struct  gxcr_ipsec_sa_t
 Data structure that encapsulates a pointer to the memory that is to be used with shim for the metadata, and the information about where the token and the context record fields are within that memory. More...

Modules

 IPsec ESP Inbound (gxcr/tokens/esp_ip4v_tunnel_ibound.h)
 The ESP IPv4 Tunneling Inbound token decapsulates an IPv4 packet.
 IPsec ESP Outbound (gxcr/tokens/esp_ip4v_tunnel_obound.h)
 The ESP IPv4 Tunneling Outbound token encapsulates an IPv4 packet.

Functions

void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_key (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the cipher key data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_key_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the cipher key length for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.
void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_iv (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the initialization vector data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_iv_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the length of the initialization vector for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.
void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest0 (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest0_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.
void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest1 (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest1_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.
void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_spi (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the SPI field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_spi_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the length of the SPI field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.
void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the sequence number field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the length of the sequence number field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.
void * gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum_mask (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the sequence number mask field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.
int gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum_mask_len (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns the length of the sequence number mask field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.
gxcr_result_token_t * gxcr_ipsec_result (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa)
 Returns a pointer to the result token associated with this IPsec SA.
size_t gxcr_ipsec_calc_sa_bytes (gxcr_ipsec_params_t *ipsec_params)
 Function to find out how much memory to allocate for a particular ipsec_sa.
int gxcr_ipsec_init_sa (gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa, void *metadata_mem, int metadata_mem_len, gxcr_ipsec_params_t *ipsec_params, unsigned char *key, unsigned char *iv)
 Function to initialize an ipsec_sa with cipher/digest algorithms.
size_t gxcr_ipsec_precalc_calc_memory_size (gxcr_ipsec_params_t *ipsec_params, int key_len)
 Function to find out how much MiCA-registered memory to allocate for use by gxcr_ipsec_precalc() for the purpose of performing the precalculation step needed when using HMAC or XCBC digests.
int gxcr_ipsec_precalc (gxio_mica_context_t *mica_context, gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *ipsec_sa, void *scratch_mem, int scratch_mem_len, void *digest_key, int digest_key_len)
 This function uses the MiCA accelerator to perform further initialization of the gxcr_ipsec_sa_t data structure, when either an HMAC or XCBC digest is used.

Function Documentation

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_key ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the cipher key data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_key_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the cipher key length for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_iv ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the initialization vector data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_iv_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the length of the initialization vector for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a cipher.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest0 ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest0_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest1 ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the calculated digest data for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, NULL if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_digest1_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the length of the digest for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t, 0 if this gxcr_ipsec_sa_t doesn't do a hash.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_spi ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the SPI field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_spi_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the length of the SPI field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the sequence number field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the length of the sequence number field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

void* gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum_mask ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the sequence number mask field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

int gxcr_ipsec_sa_seqnum_mask_len ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns the length of the sequence number mask field for a gxcr_ipsec_sa_t.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.

gxcr_result_token_t* gxcr_ipsec_result ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa  ) 

Returns a pointer to the result token associated with this IPsec SA.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the IPsec SA.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c.

size_t gxcr_ipsec_calc_sa_bytes ( gxcr_ipsec_params_t *  ipsec_params  ) 

Function to find out how much memory to allocate for a particular ipsec_sa.

Parameters:
ipsec_params Parameters for the definition of the IPsec SA.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

int gxcr_ipsec_init_sa ( gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa,
void *  metadata_mem,
int  metadata_mem_len,
gxcr_ipsec_params_t *  ipsec_params,
unsigned char *  key,
unsigned char *  iv 
)

Function to initialize an ipsec_sa with cipher/digest algorithms.

This sets up the metadata memory with the token and the context record, and sets up internal data structures with information on the sizes and offsets of fields in the context record so they can be accessed via accessor functions.

Parameters:
ipsec_sa Pointer to the context that will be initialized.
metadata_mem Points to memory for metadata to be used with shim. This memory must be registered with the shim.
metadata_mem_len Length, in bytes, of metadata memory.
ipsec_params Specifies cipher, digest, and other params pertaining to IPsec operation to perform on packet.
key Pointer to memory containing initial value for the key. Length is determined by the algorithms selected in the params.
iv Pointer to memory containing initial value for IV. Length is determined by the algorithms selected in the params.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

size_t gxcr_ipsec_precalc_calc_memory_size ( gxcr_ipsec_params_t *  ipsec_params,
int  key_len 
)

Function to find out how much MiCA-registered memory to allocate for use by gxcr_ipsec_precalc() for the purpose of performing the precalculation step needed when using HMAC or XCBC digests.

Parameters:
ipsec_params Parameters for the definition of the IPsec SA.
key_len Length of the digest key for this algorithm.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.

int gxcr_ipsec_precalc ( gxio_mica_context_t *  mica_context,
gxcr_ipsec_sa_t *  ipsec_sa,
void *  scratch_mem,
int  scratch_mem_len,
void *  digest_key,
int  digest_key_len 
)

This function uses the MiCA accelerator to perform further initialization of the gxcr_ipsec_sa_t data structure, when either an HMAC or XCBC digest is used.

This function must be run before the gxcr_ipsec_sa_t is used for a packet operation if one of these digest types is used. It must not be run if a regular digest will not be used.

Parameters:
mica_context An initalized MiCA context.
ipsec_sa Pointer to an SA that has been initialized via gxcr_ipsec_init_sa().
scratch_mem Pointer to scratch memory to be used by the shim. Memory must be 32-bit aligned and registed with the MiCA shim via gxio_mica_register_page(). The function gxcr_ipsec_precalc_calc_memory_size() tells how much MiCA-registered memory must be provided to this function. The memory may be freed after this function returns.
scratch_mem_len Length of provided precalc metadata memory, in bytes.
digest_key Pointer to the digest key.
digest_key_len The length, in bytes, of the digest key.
Examples:
mica/ipsec/app.c, and mica/queue/app.c.
